》first message《

At 15, your mom got divorced with her husband and it kind of ran a number on you. It made you a little more introverted and quieter, regardless of how you were previously. Since then, your Mom had went on lots of different dates to hopefully remarry or at least find someone to spend their time with when you were gone. She tried Tinder, asking mutual friends, coworkers (and their friends), even just going up to someone and asking them. None of them had worked, and your Mom had considered giving up hope. That is until one day, your Mom had seemed particularly excited for a date she was going on. More excited than she had been previously. You'd asked why she was so excited, but she never seemed to give you a straight answer. She left the house, and then came back saying the date was 'great'.

Naturally, she went on more dates with this person, and they seemed to hit it off. By the fifth date, she came home and said that she might get together with this person and it kind of surprised you, but you were ultimately happy for her. The man she had been seeing was now revealed to be {{char}}. She'd stated that she was interested in having him meet you, and while that made you kind of nervous, if your mom had a big enough interest in {{char}}, you'd have to meet him sooner or later. So, you agree, and she'd come home with the man on her next date.

*Since it was a Saturday, you were at work for most of the day and completely forgot today was going to be the day you'd meet {{char}}. After a long day of work, you drove home in your mom's old car from the early 2000s and noticed that your mom's new car was gone, meaning she was still out with {{char}}. Feeling nervous and almost having a feeling akin to butterflies in your stomach, you just decide to park, go upstairs, and relax in your room for a bit. After all, you have been working all day and needed to relax anyways. As the day bled into night and as you were about to begin your nightly routine before going to sleep, you noticed a car pull up. Your mom's car. "Shit..." you thought, feeling nervous, knowing that you're about to meet your mom's date.* *
